seo.serviceDescription: Create professional estimates and quotes. Get client approval online and convert to invoices with one click.

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between an estimate and a quote?
seoFaqs.answer: An estimate is an approximate cost for a project that may change as work progresses. A quote is a fixed price commitment that typically cannot be changed once accepted. Estimates are more common for complex projects where the full scope isn't yet defined.

seoFaqs.question: Can I convert an estimate to an invoice?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. With Invoicemonk, you can convert an approved estimate to an invoice with one click. All the line items, amounts, and client details carry over automatically, saving you time and reducing errors.

seoFaqs.question: How do I write a professional estimate?
seoFaqs.answer: A professional estimate should include your business details, the client's information, a detailed breakdown of services or products, individual and total costs, validity period, and payment terms. Using estimate software ensures consistent, branded documents every time.

seoFaqs.question: Should I charge for estimates?
seoFaqs.answer: It depends on your industry and the complexity of the work. Simple estimates are typically free, but detailed proposals requiring site visits or research may warrant a fee. Charging for estimates can also filter out non-serious inquiries.

faq.title: Estimates questions, answered
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about creating and managing estimates.

items.question: What's the difference between an estimate and an invoice?
items.answer: An estimate (or quote) is a proposal for work before it's done, while an invoice is a bill for work that's been completed. With Invoicemonk, you can easily convert approved estimates into invoices.

items.question: Can clients approve estimates online?
items.answer: Yes! When you send an estimate, your client receives a link where they can review the details and approve with a single click. You'll be notified immediately when they approve.

items.question: Can I set expiration dates on estimates?
items.answer: Absolutely. You can set validity periods for your quotes, and we'll notify you before they expire. This helps create urgency and keeps your pipeline moving.

items.question: How do I convert an estimate to an invoice?
items.answer: Once a client approves your estimate, you can convert it to an invoice with one click. All the line items, pricing, and client details transfer automatically.

items.question: Can I track which estimates are pending?
items.answer: Yes, your dashboard shows all estimates organized by status — draft, sent, viewed, approved, or expired. You'll always know where each proposal stands.
